title: STANDARD FOR QUICK FROZEN STRAWBERRIES CXS 52-1981 Adopted in 1981. Amended in 2019, 2022.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR QUICK FROZEN STRAWBERRIES CXS 52-1981 Adopted in 1981. Amended in 2019, 2022.
sections.summary: Document title: Standard for Quick Frozen Strawberries (CXS 52-1981), adopted 1981 and amended in 2019 and 2022, with a noted amendment to Section 6.5 on labelling of non-retail containers.

Applies to quick frozen strawberries (excluding puree) of Fragaria grandiflora L. and Fragaria vesca L. offered for direct consumption without further processing except for size grading or repacking, and excludes products intended for further processing or industrial use.

Describes quick frozen strawberries' product and process definitions, handling practice and permitted presentations including whole/halved/sliced/cut forms, requirements for rapid passage through the temperature range of maximum crystallization and final centre temperature, handling to maintain quality, and sizing/presentation rules.
Defines quick frozen strawberries as products prepared from fresh, clean, sound, ripe and stemmed strawberries of firm texture conforming to Fragaria grandiflora L. or Fragaria vesca L.

Requires freezing in appropriate equipment so the temperature range of maximum crystallization is passed quickly and the thermal centre reaches -18 °C after stabilization, and permits repacking under controlled conditions.

Requires the product to be handled during transportation, storage and distribution so as to maintain quality up to final sale and recommends following the Code of Practice for the Processing and Handling of Quick Frozen Foods (CXC 8-1976).

Specifies permitted presentations (whole, halved, sliced or cut; free-flowing or non free-flowing) and allows other distinctive presentations provided they meet the standard and are clearly described on the label, and includes sizing requirements.

Allows presentation as whole, halved, sliced or cut and as free‑flowing or non free‑flowing, and permits other styles only if sufficiently distinctive, compliant with the standard and adequately described on the label.

Permits whole strawberries to be sized or unsized; if size graded the largest berry's diameter must not exceed the smallest by more than 10 mm, and for Fragaria grandiflora L. each berry must have a maximum diameter of at least 15 mm.

Establishes essential composition and quality requirements including permitted sugars, soluble solids limits for dry-sugar and syrup preparations, organoleptic and analytical quality criteria, definitions of defects with numeric tolerances based on a 500 g sample, size tolerances, defective classification and lot acceptance using an AQL 6.5 sampling plan.
Lists permitted optional sweetening ingredients: sucrose, invert sugar, dextrose, fructose, glucose syrup and dried glucose syrup.

Sets soluble solids limits: for strawberries prepared with dry sugars the thawed sample's liquid must have 18–35% m/m (as sucrose), and for syrup‑prepared strawberries the liquid must have 15–25% m/m (as sucrose) with syrup only sufficient to cover berries and fill spaces.

Specifies organoleptic and other quality factors (good colour, absence of foreign flavours/odours, intactness, cleanliness, freedom from stalks/extraneous material, soundness, varietal uniformity, appropriate free‑flowing behaviour) and an analytical limit for mineral impurities (max 0.1% m/m).

Specifies organoleptic and integrity requirements for quick frozen strawberries: they must be of good colour, free from foreign flavours/odours, intact (whole or cut) and not materially disintegrated, clean and practically free from sand/grit and extraneous vegetable material or stalk parts, sound (practically free from mould, insect bites and blemishes), normally developed, varietally uniform, and when sold as free‑flowing practically free from berries adhering to one another and not icy.
Limits mineral impurities (e.g., sand) to no more than 0.1% m/m on a whole-product basis.

Defines defect terms for strawberries with numeric or descriptive criteria: 'partially uncoloured' (25–75% of outer surface uncoloured), 'completely uncoloured' (≥75%), 'disintegrated' (broken/crushed/smashed), 'stalks or parts of stalks' (>3 mm in one dimension), 'blemished' (visible pest or injury), and 'misshapen' (not normally developed, may contain hard parts).

For a 500 g drained fruit sample, specifies maximum tolerances: stalks/parts >3 mm — 3 by number; calyces — 3 cm²; other extraneous vegetable material — 3 cm²; completely uncoloured whole berries — 1 by number; partially uncoloured whole berries — 5% m/m; disintegrated whole berries — 5% m/m; blemished — 5% m/m; misshapen whole berries — 2% m/m; dissimilar varieties — 6% m/m.

Defines the 'drained fruit ingredient' method: thaw until practically free of ice crystals, drain on a 3 mesh/cm (8 mesh/inch) screen for two minutes and weigh retained fruit (adding any stalks/calyces/extraneous vegetable material found in the drained syrup); if dry sugar was added after freezing, wash it off before draining.

sections.section: 3.6 Tolerances for sizes of whole strawberries
sections.summary: Size tolerances: when marketed as sized, up to 10% by number may fail the size requirement of paragraph 2.4.2(b), and for Fragaria grandiflora L. no more than 5% by number may have maximum diameter less than 15 mm.

Defines a sample unit (AQL 6.5) as 'defective' if soluble solids fall outside specified ranges, any organoleptic characteristic in 3.3.1 is not met, any single listed defect exceeds twice its tolerance, the total of defects (e–i) exceeds 15% for whole berries (or defects g and i exceed 12% for halved/sliced/cut berries), or size tolerances are exceeded.

States that a lot is acceptable when the number of defectives (as defined in 3.7) for each defect category, treated independently, does not exceed the acceptance number 'c' of an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5.
Permits ascorbic acid and citric acid as food additives for the product, with maximum levels limited by Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP).

Recommends preparing and handling the product per the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene and related codes; the product should, to the extent practicable in GMP, be free from objectionable matter and, when tested, free from microorganisms, parasites, or microbial substances in amounts that may pose a health hazard.
Heading noting that, beyond the General Standard for Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods, additional specific labelling provisions for this product follow.

Labelling obligations: the name must include 'strawberries' (or 'wild/alpine strawberries' for Fragaria vesca L.) and 'quick frozen' (or 'frozen' where customary), and must indicate style ('halves', 'slices', 'cut') and packing medium (naming any sweetener or syrup); additional qualifying wording is required when the product presentation might mislead (per subsection 2.4.1.c).

If a size-designating term is used on the label it must be supported by a correct graphic size representation and/or a statement of the predominant maximum diameter in millimetres (or customary local units) or conform to the country's customary size-declaration method.
Retail packs must include instructions for keeping and thawing the product.

For bulk quick frozen strawberries, required information must appear on the container or accompanying documents, but the name with 'quick frozen' (or 'frozen' where allowed) and the manufacturer/packer name and address must appear on the container.
States that labelling of non‑retail containers should comply with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346‑2021).

Packaging requirements: packaging must protect the product's organoleptic and quality characteristics, prevent bacteriological and other contamination (including from packaging material), prevent moisture loss/dehydration and leakage as far as technologically practicable, and must not impart odour, taste, colour or other foreign characteristics.

sections.section: 7.1 Packaging used for quick frozen strawberries must
sections.summary: Restates that packaging for quick frozen strawberries must protect organoleptic/quality characteristics, guard against contamination (including from packaging), prevent moisture loss/dehydration/leakage as practicable, and not transfer odour, taste, colour or other foreign characteristics.

sections.section: 7.1.1 protect the organoleptic and quality characteristics of the product
sections.summary: Specifies that packaging must protect the organoleptic and quality characteristics of quick frozen strawberries.

sections.section: 7.1.2 protect  the  product  from  bacteriological  and  other  contamination  (including  contamination  from  the packaging material itself)
sections.summary: Specifies that packaging must protect the product from bacteriological and other contamination, including contamination originating from the packaging material itself.

sections.section: 7.1.3 protect the  product from moisture  loss,  dehydration  and,  where  appropriate,  leakage  as far as technologically practicable
sections.summary: Packaging shall protect the product from moisture loss, dehydration and, where appropriate, leakage to the extent technologically practicable.

sections.section: 7.1.4 not pass on to the product any odour, taste, colour or other foreign characteristics
sections.summary: Packaging materials shall not impart any odour, taste, colour or other foreign characteristics to the product.

Methods of analysis and sampling for checking compliance with this standard shall be those contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions of the standard.
